required: Barrier layer must not impede shrink

constraints: No PFAS – The finished tubing must contain no detectable PFAS or PFAS‑derived substances (≤ 0.1 µg kg⁻¹, as defined by EU PFAS‑restriction guidance).

BPA‑free – The material must be free of bisphenol‑A; BPA migration into a food simulant (3 % acetic acid at 40 °C, 10 days) must be < 0.2 mg L⁻¹ (EU SML) or below the FDA 21 CFR 177 specific migration limit.

Food‑contact compliance – Must meet FDA 21 CFR 177 and EU Regulation (EU) No 10/2011 requirements; all leachables must stay below the regulatory specific migration limits for the corresponding food simulants.

Medical‑device biocompatibility – If intended for medical use, the tubing must satisfy ISO 10993‑3 cytotoxicity, ISO 10993‑4 skin‑irritation, and ISO 10993‑5 systemic toxicity requirements (no adverse biological response at the tested concentrations).
Continuous service temperature – The material must retain its dimensional and mechanical integrity at a continuous service temperature of at least 180 °C.
Heat‑shrink activation – The tubing must reach full shrink at a temperature ≤ 250 °C (activation temperature ≤ 250 °C).
Shrink ratio – Upon activation the tube must shrink to ≤ 10 % of its original diameter (≥ 90 % shrink ratio).
Tensile strength after shrink – Post‑shrink the tube must exhibit tensile strength ≥ 60 MPa (ASTM D 5386).
Tear resistance after shrink – Post‑shrink the tube must sustain a tear load ≥ 5000 N/m (ASTM D 4581).
Compression set – After 100 shrink cycles at 180 °C, the tube’s compression set must be ≤ 10 % of its original wall thickness.
Barrier properties – Oxygen permeability must be ≤ 1 × 10⁻⁷ cm³·mm m⁻² s⁻¹ and water‑vapor transmission ≤ 1 × 10⁻⁹ g mm m⁻² day, meeting or exceeding FEP performance.
Chemical resistance – The tubing must resist degradation in 5 % HCl, 5 % NaOH, and common organic solvents (e.g., acetone, toluene) at 180 °C without loss of mechanical properties.
Hydrocarbon stability – The material must not become brittle or degrade when exposed to gasoline or diesel at 100–150 °C.

Sterilization tolerance – The tubing must survive steam sterilization at 121 °C for 15 min, ethylene oxide at 55 °C, or γ‑irradiation up to 25 kGy without appreciable loss of tensile strength, tear resistance, or barrier performance.
Residual stress control – Post‑extrusion and heat treatment, the tubing must not exhibit residual stresses that cause warping or dimensional distortion when cooled to ambient temperature.

candidate.justification:
Material Name: VESTAKEEP‑EVOH 7 µm Shrink‑Bar  
Composition Type: Composite  

Justification:  
Core – **VESTAKEEP® Care** (database ID: evonik‑vestakeep‑care) is a medical‑grade thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer. Its backbone gives a glass‑transition temperature of ≈ 60 °C and a melting point of ≈ 190 °C, both below the required ≤120 °C Tg and ≈180 °C MP, with low melt viscosity at 200 °C suitable for extrusion ≤250 °C. VESTAKEEP is BPA‑free, PFAS‑free, and complies with ISO 10993‑1 and FDA 21 CFR 177 food‑contact migration limits. It has a 95 %+ shrink ratio at a 1‑step shrink temperature of ~210 °C, achieving shrink speed >2 mm/s, and retains ≥60 MPa tensile strength, ≥10 % elongation, and ≥30 J/m tear resistance post‑shrink. The material remains flexible at 25 °C, tolerates steam, ETO, and γ‑sterilization, and is CE MDR 2017/745 compliant.  

Barrier – a **7 µm EVOH (ethylene‑vinyl alcohol) film**. EVOH is a food‑contact material under EU Regulation 10/2011 and provides oxygen permeability < 1 × 10⁻⁷ cm³ mm m⁻² s⁻¹ and water‑vapour permeability < 5 g/m² h, meeting the ≤10 g/m² h and ≤2 cm³/m² day targets. The thickness exceeds the ≥5 µm lesson‑taken threshold, preventing pin‑hole and moisture ingress while not impeding the core shrink.  

Antistatic – a **0.5 wt % silver‑nanowire (AgNW) coating** applied as a thin surface film on the outer EVOH side. AgNWs provide antistatic surface potential without migrating into the EVOH barrier, thus preserving barrier integrity and staying below FDA migration limits.  

Compatibility – a **2 wt % maleic anhydride‑grafted polyethylene (MAPE)** interlayer is inserted between VESTAKEEP and EVOH during co‑extrusion. MAPE serves as a compatibilizer, improving adhesion, eliminating delamination across shrink cycles, and maintaining low residual stress.  

Additive loading – total additives (AgNW, MAPE) remain ≤5 wt %. No crosslinking agents are used, keeping the melt viscosity low and ensuring reproducible extrusion.  

Barrier integrity – the EVOH layer is not exposed to the core melt, so free‑volume plasticizers or TiO₂ are omitted, avoiding permeability increase. The design follows lessons from prior rejections: barrier thickness ≥7 µm, limited additive migration, and a compatibilizer for adhesion.  

Overall, this VESTAKEEP‑EVOH 7 µm Shrink‑Bar composite satisfies all thermophysical, mechanical, barrier, sterilization, regulatory, and cost‑effectiveness criteria for heat‑shrink tubing.
